message:
fix(distributed): Harden security error responses to avoid information leakage

Tightens distributed handshake error responses to only expose information
relevant to the specific error type:

- Refactor distributed_publish_security_error to conditionally include
  protocol_version only for ProtocolVersionMissing and ProtocolVersionMismatch
  errors, where the client needs it for version negotiation
- Remove server_version from all error responses to prevent server
  fingerprinting by unauthenticated clients
- Auth failure responses no longer include protocol_version, preventing
  enumeration of protocol capabilities before authentication

Test updates:
- Assert auth_failed responses contain neither protocol_version nor
  server_version (information leakage prevention)
- Assert version mismatch responses include protocol_version but not
  server_version (minimal disclosure principle)

Also updates .cargo/config.toml.example and beads tracker metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.2","title":"[ops] Real-time dashboards and alerting for fleet health","description":"Deliver operational dashboards and alerting policies for swarm health, queue saturation, connector failures, policy denials, and recovery outcomes with actionable routing and runbook linkage.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Dashboards expose actionable state (health, saturation, failures, policy denials, recoveries), not raw noise.
2. Alerts are tuned to reduce false positives/negatives and include severity-based routing/escalation.
3. Every alert class links to runbook guidance and captures operator ack/resolve trails.
4. Includes comprehensive unit/integration/e2e dashboard+alert tests with detailed structured logs/artifacts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:28.496447Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T20:01:18.187773Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alerts","dashboards","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:38:29.634935Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:28.496447Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:26.175188Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3","title":"[ops] Capacity governor with rch-aware heavy workload control","description":"Implement workload governance that detects pressure and routes or throttles heavy compile/test operations (using rch for offload) to avoid local contention and collapse under swarm load.","acceptance_criteria":"1. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:32.663926Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:38:30.540921Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capacity","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:27.121737Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:24.484356Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:38:30.540869Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:32.663926Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:26.639495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","title":"[ops][rch] Fix remote-exec fallback when rch sync succeeds but remote exec fails and falls open local","description":"Observed in /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm during compile-unblock work: rch selects workers and completes sync, then remote command execution fails with 'Failed to connect to ubuntu@<host>' and falls back to local execution ([RCH] local). Repro: rch exec -- cargo check --help. In contrast, manual ssh and rch workers capabilities --refresh succeed for all workers. This blocks policy-compliant offloaded cargo validation for active beads. Goal: identify and fix exec-path connectivity mismatch, restore reliable remote execution, and capture validation evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"OliveCreek","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:30:28.539385Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T04:57:32.654612Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","infrastructure","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:30:28.539385Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1847,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"ChartreuseFox","text":"Taking non-overlap slice in-repo (scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh + tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh). Reproduced fail-open path: rch check/status healthy, workers probe all RCH-E100, rch exec syncs then logs 'Remote execution failed ... running locally' and emits '[RCH] local'. Implementing stricter validator + e2e guard for remote-exec failure signature.","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:39:44Z"},{"id":1848,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Completed non-overlap guardrail slice:
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh: heavy runs now require fallback metadata when worker_context indicates local/fallback even if used_rch=true (captures rch fail-open cases).
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh self-test extended with fail-open negative/positive coverage.
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh: added RCH fail-open regex guard ("[RCH] local", "Remote execution failed: ... running locally", "Failed to connect to ubuntu@").
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh: added preflight rch remote smoke step (rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/tmp cargo --version) and hard-fail on fail-open signature before heavy test/check steps.
Validation:
- bash scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh --self-test  => pass
- bash -n scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh => pass
- bash tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh => exits 2 with 'no reachable rch workers; refusing local fallback' (expected current infra state)
Artifacts:
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260304_024259.jsonl
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260304_024259.stdout.log","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:43:36Z"},{"id":1981,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DarkCliff assist 2026-03-07: added a local-side macOS guardrail for the rch fail-open bug in this repo's existing policy harness.

Findings:
- Reproduced a distinct local-side root cause not covered by the earlier remote-failure-only guard: when local TMPDIR is a long /var/folders/... path on macOS, rch's SSH ControlMaster socket path exceeds the Unix socket limit.
- Symptom: workers probe / remote exec can fail even though manual SSH works; rch then risks fail-open local execution.
- Confirmed workaround: prefix local rch invocations with TMPDIR=/tmp. Setting TMPDIR only inside the remote command is insufficient because the failure happens in the local rch SSH wrapper.

Changes:
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh now routes every rch invocation through a Darwin-aware wrapper that applies TMPDIR=/tmp locally, logs that workaround, and broadens fail-open detection to include the socket-path-too-long signature.
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh self-test now covers env-prefixed rch commands (TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- ...) so policy classification continues to treat them as compliant rch usage.

Validation:
- bash -n scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh ✅
- bash scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh --self-test ✅
","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:49:27Z"},{"id":1983,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DarkCliff follow-up 2026-03-07: corrected the preflight smoke in tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh from `rch exec -- cargo --version` to `rch exec -- cargo check --help`.

Why:
- `cargo --version` is classified by rch as a non-compilation command and can run locally, so it does not prove remote exec.
- `cargo check --help` stays lightweight but exercises the actual remote compilation execution path.

Live verification:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help` selected worker `vmi1149989`, synced the project, executed remotely, and completed with `[RCH] remote vmi1149989`.
","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:51:31Z"},{"id":2037,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"WhiteFox","text":"Additional `rch` failure mode reproduced while validating `ft-3681t.7.3.2`.

Evidence:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_whitefox_check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --message-format short` succeeded remotely on `vmi1149989` (`[RCH] remote vmi1149989`).
- Repeated targeted remote test commands (`cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib command_uses_rch` and `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib robot_heavy_cargo_without_rch_requires_approval`) were scheduled to `vmi1227854` and failed immediately with remote exit 101 before compilation.
- Failure signature on `vmi1227854`:
  `failed to read /home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml`

Interpretation:
- This looks like worker-topology drift / inconsistent sibling-repo provisioning across the fleet: some workers (at least `vmi1149989`) have the required `frankenredis` sibling tree, while others (`vmi1227854`) do not.
- This is distinct from the macOS TMPDIR/SSH-control-socket workaround: even with `TMPDIR=/tmp`, remote execution can still fail if the selected worker lacks the expected sibling repo layout.

Potential follow-up:
- audit which workers have `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/...` populated
- make worker topology consistent or teach `rch` selection to avoid workers missing required sibling repos for this project
- revisit `.rch/config.toml` exclusions and remote path expectations so path dependencies are not silently worker-dependent.
","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:56:33Z"},{"id":2121,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"ProudMeadow","text":"ProudMeadow support-child `ft-3681t.7.3.1.1` landed a repo-local topology preflight in `tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh`.

Important updated finding from live validation: the worker-layout mismatch is broader than the earlier `frankenredis` report. The new per-worker preflight shows all configured workers currently missing the remote `frankentui` sibling manifests expected by this checkout (first failing path: `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankentui/crates/ftui-backend/Cargo.toml`).

Artifact bundle:
- `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617.jsonl`
- `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617_rch_external_path_deps.txt`
- `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617_rch_worker_topology.jsonl`

Net effect: the existing guard now fails at preflight with `RCH-REMOTE-TOPOLOGY` before any heavy cargo step can wander into a misleading late-stage remote/local fallback path.","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:57:32Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.1","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3.1][support] Preflight remote path-dependency topology before heavy rch runs","description":"Implement a bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3.1: extend the existing repo-local rch guard harness to detect remote worker topology drift for Cargo path dependencies before any heavy cargo command is launched. Scope: existing shell guard/test surfaces only; no rch upstream changes. Files should stay limited to tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh and any directly-related local policy validator updates if needed.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:52:14.259827Z","created_by":"ProudMeadow","updated_at":"2026-03-11T04:57:32.672725Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","infrastructure","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:52:14.259827Z","created_by":"ProudMeadow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2122,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.1","author":"ProudMeadow","text":"Implemented bounded shell-only support slice in `tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh` for remote path-dependency topology preflight.

What changed:
- Added `python3`-backed scan of repo `Cargo.toml` files to collect external path dependencies that resolve outside the repo root.
- Added worker inventory loader from `~/.config/rch/workers.toml` and direct SSH preflight over every configured worker.
- Added per-worker artifact logs plus a JSONL topology report before any heavy `rch exec -- cargo ...` step runs.
- The existing e2e guard now exits early with `RCH-REMOTE-TOPOLOGY` when any worker is missing required remote sibling manifests.

Validation:
- `bash -n tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh` ✅
- `git diff --check -- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh` ✅
- `bash tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh` -> exits 2 at the new preflight as intended, before heavy remote cargo work or any local fallback

Artifacts:
- `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617.jsonl`
- `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617_rch_external_path_deps.txt`
- `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617_rch_worker_topology.jsonl`
- per-worker logs: `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617_<worker>_topology.log`

Key finding:
- The current topology mismatch is fleet-wide for the `frankentui` sibling manifests, not just the previously observed single-worker `frankenredis` symptom. In this run every configured worker reported missing `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankentui/crates/ftui-backend/Cargo.toml` during preflight, so the harness now fails fast with a precise artifact instead of drifting into later remote-exec/local-fallback confusion.","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:57:32Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.1","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3.1][support] Preflight remote path-dependency topology before heavy rch runs","description":"Implement a bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3.1: extend the existing repo-local rch guard harness to detect remote worker topology drift for Cargo path dependencies before any heavy cargo command is launched. Scope: existing shell guard/test surfaces only; no rch upstream changes. Files should stay limited to tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh and any directly-related local policy validator updates if needed.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaDesert","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:52:14.259827Z","created_by":"ProudMeadow","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:03:34.035815Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","infrastructure","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:52:14.259827Z","created_by":"ProudMeadow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2122,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.1","author":"ProudMeadow","text":"Implemented bounded shell-only support slice in `tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh` for remote path-dependency topology preflight.

What changed:
- Added `python3`-backed scan of repo `Cargo.toml` files to collect external path dependencies that resolve outside the repo root.
- Added worker inventory loader from `~/.config/rch/workers.toml` and direct SSH preflight over every configured worker.
- Added per-worker artifact logs plus a JSONL topology report before any heavy `rch exec -- cargo ...` step runs.
- The existing e2e guard now exits early with `RCH-REMOTE-TOPOLOGY` when any worker is missing required remote sibling manifests.

Validation:
- `bash -n tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh` ✅
- `git diff --check -- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh` ✅
- `bash tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh` -> exits 2 at the new preflight as intended, before heavy remote cargo work or any local fallback

Artifacts:
- `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617.jsonl`
- `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617_rch_external_path_deps.txt`
- `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617_rch_worker_topology.jsonl`
- per-worker logs: `tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260311_045617_<worker>_topology.log`

Key finding:
- The current topology mismatch is fleet-wide for the `frankentui` sibling manifests, not just the previously observed single-worker `frankenredis` symptom. In this run every configured worker reported missing `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankentui/crates/ftui-backend/Cargo.toml` during preflight, so the harness now fails fast with a precise artifact instead of drifting into later remote-exec/local-fallback confusion.","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:57:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.2","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3.1][support] Replace FrankenRedis sibling path deps with pinned git deps for deterministic rch workers","description":"Bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3.1. Replace the optional FrankenRedis dependencies in crates/frankenterm-core from sibling path references (../../../frankenredis/...) to pinned git dependencies so remote rch workers do not require an out-of-repo frankenredis checkout. Preserve local development convenience via config.toml.example override guidance, update Cargo.lock, and validate the previously worker-dependent remote cargo test path through rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:54:20.378040Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:13.376532Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:13.374930Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","infrastructure","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:54:20.378040Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2125,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented the bounded manifest/root-cause slice.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml`: replaced the optional FrankenRedis sibling path deps (`../../../frankenredis/...`) with pinned git deps against `https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenredis` at `rev 7b00450d924e8048073745736356ae0e45a592af`.
- `Cargo.lock`: path packages for `fr-config`, `fr-expire`, `fr-persist`, `fr-protocol`, and `fr-store` now resolve to that git source.
- `.cargo/config.toml.example`: added a local override stanza so developers with a sibling checkout can still patch the same crates back to local paths intentionally.

Why:
- WhiteFox's prior worker failure (`failed to read .../frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml`) was caused by manifest-level path assumptions inside this repo rather than by the worker's Cargo/test invocation itself.
- After the manifest change, two different `rch` workers resolved FrankenRedis through git instead of requiring an out-of-repo sibling checkout.

Validation (all offloaded through `rch` where compilation/test work was involved):
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/brightoak-rch-ft-3681t-7-3-1-2-test cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib command_uses_rch -- --nocapture` -> remote success on `vmi1152480`; test `build_coord::tests::command_uses_rch_detects_common_prefix_shapes` passed.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/brightoak-rch-ft-3681t-7-3-1-2-check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --features redis-session --message-format short` -> remote success on `vmi1149989` with exit 0 after fetching and compiling the git-pinned FrankenRedis crates.
- `git diff --check -- crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml .cargo/config.toml.example Cargo.lock` -> clean.

Residual note:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` is classified by `rch` as non-compilation and did not provide a meaningful remote validation artifact, so I did not count it as evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.3","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3.1][support] Replace Frankentui sibling path deps with pinned git deps for deterministic rch workers","description":"Bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3.1. Replace the optional Frankentui subcrate dependencies in crates/frankenterm-core from sibling path references (../../../frankentui/...) to pinned git dependencies so remote rch workers do not require an out-of-repo frankentui checkout. Preserve local development convenience via config.toml.example override guidance, update Cargo.lock, and validate a remote feature path that resolves the ftui subcrates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:48.393096Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T05:14:04.884693Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T05:14:04.884638Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","infrastructure","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:48.393096Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2126,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented the bounded Frankentui manifest slice.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml`: replaced the optional Frankentui subcrate sibling path deps (`../../../frankentui/...`) with pinned git deps against `https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankentui` at `rev 18facd3f2a2c51b8b06ff589e6b905e6a63f5a96`.
- `.cargo/config.toml.example`: extended the existing Frankentui patch block so local sibling-checkout development can still intentionally override `ftui`, `ftui-core`, `ftui-render`, `ftui-layout`, `ftui-style`, `ftui-widgets`, `ftui-runtime`, and `ftui-backend`.

Why:
- ProudMeadow's topology preflight artifact showed the current first missing remote manifest was `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankentui/crates/ftui-backend/Cargo.toml`, indicating a second ambient sibling-layout assumption after the FrankenRedis fix.

Validation:
- `git diff --check -- crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml .cargo/config.toml.example Cargo.lock` -> clean.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/brightoak-rch-ft-3681t-7-3-1-3-check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --features tui-dashboard --message-format short` -> remote execution on `vmi1152480` reached and resolved the Frankentui git crates successfully (`ftui-core`, `ftui-render`, `ftui-layout`, `ftui-style`, `ftui-widgets`, `ftui-runtime`, `ftui-backend` all appeared as git-sourced in the remote check output).
- That remote check then failed with unrelated pre-existing `policy_diagnostics.rs` compile errors (`KillSwitch.level`, missing telemetry fields, missing `snapshot` methods) rather than any missing Frankentui path-manifest error.

Residual note:
- I did not modify `Cargo.lock` for this slice because the workspace was already carrying the same pinned Frankentui git revision; the lockfile still contains duplicate `ftui-*` entries, but the active manifest path used by the remote worker resolved through the git source rather than a sibling checkout.

Keep scope to policy/build_coord/tests only.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteFox","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:56:33.245598Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2036,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","author":"WhiteFox","text":"Implemented the bounded policy/build_coord slice.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs`: added `command_uses_rch`, `is_heavy_cargo_command`, `requires_rch_offload`, and `recommended_rch_prefix()`; the parser now handles env-prefixed commands and `rch exec -- cargo ...` wrappers.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs`: added `policy.rch.heavy_compute` so non-human `SendText` requests invoking heavy cargo without `rch` now return `RequireApproval` with an `rch` prefix recommendation.
- Added focused unit tests covering wrapped/unwrapped heavy cargo detection and policy decisions.

Validation:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help` -> remote success on `vmi1149989`.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_whitefox_check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --message-format short` -> remote success on `vmi1149989` in 2m07s (`[RCH] remote vmi1149989`).
- Initial remote compile exposed an implementation bug (`shell_words::split` unresolved in `build_coord.rs`); fixed by switching to existing `shlex::split`, then reran the remote check successfully.

Remaining blocker:
- Targeted remote `cargo test` commands for the new unit tests are currently blocked by worker topology drift: scheduler repeatedly sends them to `vmi1227854`, which exits 101 because `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml` is missing on that worker. Implementation is complete; remote test execution is infra-blocked rather than code-blocked.
